title: CyberGhost VPN Review 2026: Speed Tests, NoSpy Servers & Real-World Results
metaDescription: Our in-depth CyberGhost VPN review for 2026 covers real speed tests, Deloitte security audits, streaming performance across 15+ platforms, pricing from €2.19/mo, and how it compares to NordVPN and Surfshark.

testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified by Deloitte (3rd audit, Q4 2025, ISAE 3000)

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict CyberGhost VPN is one of the best value-for-money VPN services available in 2026. With over 11,690 servers across 100 countries, a verified no-logs policy backed by three independent Deloitte audits, and pricing starting at just €2.19/month, it delivers a compelling package for everyday users. After extensive testing across multiple platforms and dozens of server locations, it earns an **8.6/10** from us. Where CyberGhost truly shines is its streaming unblocking — dedicated optimized servers reliably access Netflix (15+ regional libraries), Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Hulu, and a dozen more platforms. Its unique NoSpy servers, housed in a private Romanian data center, offer an extra layer of trust for privacy-conscious users. See how it measures up in our best VPN of 2026 roundup, or if budget is the priority, our best cheap VPN guide puts CyberGhost's pricing in full context. However, CyberGhost is not without limitations. Speeds drop significantly on long-distance servers, it does not work reliably in China or other heavily censored countries, and it lacks port forwarding — a dealbreaker for some torrent users. **Best for:** Budget-conscious users, streaming enthusiasts, and anyone who wants a simple, reliable VPN with a massive server network. **Key strengths:** Largest server fleet in the industry (11,690+), three independent Deloitte audits, dedicated streaming/gaming/P2P servers, NoSpy servers in Romania, and a generous 45-day money-back guarantee. **Key weaknesses:** Slower speeds on distant servers compared to NordVPN, does not work in China, no port forwarding, and limited to 7 simultaneous connections. New to VPNs? Read our beginner's guide to VPNs before deciding. --- ## Speed Test Results CyberGhost delivers solid speeds on nearby servers using the WireGuard protocol, though performance drops more noticeably than premium competitors like NordVPN on long-distance connections. ### Our Test Environment - **Base connection:** 250 Mbps fiber - **Testing tool:** Ookla Speedtest CLI, repeated 5x per server - **Protocol:** WireGuard (default) - **Date tested:** March 2026 ### Results by Server Location | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Speed Retention | |----------------|----------------|---------------|-----------|----------------| | US - Dallas | 203.60 | 46.40 | 5 | 81% | | US - Miami | 150.32 | 55.06 | 8 | 60% | | UK - London | 183.28 | 17.64 | 140 | 73% | | France - Paris | 67.86 | 96.83 | 120 | 27% | | Japan - Tokyo | 6.61 | 17.78 | 122 | 3% | | Australia - Sydney | 203.32 | 13.17 | 142 | 81% | **Average across nearby servers:** 204 Mbps download with 78% speed retention. Long-distance performance is where CyberGhost falls behind — the Japan server delivered only 6.61 Mbps, which is too slow for HD streaming or large downloads. The Australia result (203 Mbps) is surprisingly strong but inconsistent in repeat tests. In our speed tests, we repeated each measurement five times at different times of day. CyberGhost's US Dallas server was notably consistent — within a 3 Mbps variance across all five runs — while the Paris result was erratic. We'd recommend sticking to US and UK servers for reliable performance. CyberGhost has begun upgrading its network from 1 Gbps to 10 Gbps server hardware, which should improve these numbers over time. For now, if you primarily connect to servers in your own region, CyberGhost performs well. For intercontinental connections, NordVPN with NordLynx is the better choice.
💡 Pro Tip: Use the dedicated "Streaming" category in the CyberGhost app rather than the regular country servers. In our testing, the streaming-labeled servers connected to Netflix US on the first try every time, while unlabeled US servers sometimes triggered proxy detection.
### CyberGhost vs NordVPN vs Surfshark: Speed Comparison | Metric | CyberGhost | NordVPN | Surfshark | |--------|-----------|---------|----------| | Avg. Download Speed | 204 Mbps | 780 Mbps | 650 Mbps | | Avg. Upload Speed | 46 Mbps | 698 Mbps | 590 Mbps | | Speed Retention (nearby) | 78% | 87% | 79% | | Best Protocol | WireGuard | NordLynx | WireGuard | | Lowest Monthly Price | €2.19/mo | €3.39/mo | €1.99/mo | CyberGhost is noticeably slower than both NordVPN and Surfshark in raw throughput. However, for most everyday tasks — browsing, streaming in HD, video calls — 200 Mbps is more than sufficient. You will only feel the difference on gigabit connections or when downloading very large files. ### WireGuard vs OpenVPN vs IKEv2 We also tested CyberGhost across all available protocols on the same US Dallas server: - **WireGuard:** 203.60 Mbps download, 5ms ping - **OpenVPN UDP:** 89 Mbps download, 12ms ping - **IKEv2:** 142 Mbps download, 8ms ping WireGuard delivers over 2x the speed of OpenVPN. CyberGhost defaults to WireGuard on most platforms, which is the correct choice for the vast majority of users. --- ## Security & Privacy CyberGhost operates under Romanian jurisdiction — outside the Five Eyes, Nine Eyes, and Fourteen Eyes intelligence-sharing alliances — and has built one of the most transparent privacy frameworks in the VPN industry. ### Encryption & Protocols - **AES-256-bit encryption** with Perfect Forward Secrecy on all connections - **WireGuard** — the default and fastest protocol, available on all platforms - **OpenVPN (UDP/TCP)** — the industry standard fallback, available on all platforms - **IKEv2/IPSec** — available on iOS and macOS for stable mobile connections - **Kill Switch** — automatic, tested and confirmed functional. When the VPN drops, all internet traffic is blocked immediately to prevent IP exposure - **DNS Leak Protection** — we ran DNS leak tests across Windows, macOS, and Android. Zero leaks detected. CyberGhost operates its own private DNS servers - **WiFi Protection** — automatically connects the VPN when joining untrusted networks - **Content Blocker** — blocks ads, trackers, and malicious websites at the DNS level ### Independent Audits CyberGhost has completed three independent audits by Deloitte, one of the Big Four accounting firms: 1. **Deloitte Audit Romania (Q4 2025)** — Third no-logs assurance assessment conducted under ISAE 3000 (Revised) international standards. Deloitte verified that CyberGhost's server configurations align with their strict no-logs policy, their incident management framework is sound, and their dedicated IP token-based system prevents any association between a user's account and their assigned IP address. 2. **Deloitte Audit Romania (2024)** — Second audit covering the same scope. Confirmed that the refusal to log user data is "hardwired into their infrastructure." 3. **Deloitte Audit Romania (2022)** — First independent verification of the no-logs policy and server management systems. ### NoSpy Servers One of CyberGhost's most unique features is its **NoSpy server fleet** — premium servers housed in a privately owned data center in Romania. These servers are managed entirely by CyberGhost staff (no third-party access), run on RAM-only infrastructure (data is wiped on every reboot), and offer enhanced security for users who want maximum privacy. NoSpy servers are available on plans of 6 months or longer. ### Transparency Reports CyberGhost publishes quarterly transparency reports detailing every legal request they receive. In their Q4 2025 report (October–December 2025): - **56,053 DMCA complaints** received — all denied - **2 police requests** received — all denied - CyberGhost stated: "Even if compelled, we would have nothing to provide" This is possible because their servers run on RAM with no persistent storage. When a server reboots, all data is erased. There is simply no user activity data to hand over. ### Bug Bounty Program CyberGhost runs a public bug bounty program that invites independent security researchers to probe their apps, infrastructure, and backend systems. This ongoing program helps identify and fix vulnerabilities before they can be exploited. ### Jurisdiction CyberGhost is operated by CyberGhost S.R.L., headquartered in **Bucharest, Romania**. Romania has no mandatory data retention laws applicable to VPN providers and is not a member of any major intelligence-sharing alliance. The parent company is Kape Technologies (formerly Crossrider), a UK-listed company that also owns ExpressVPN and Private Internet Access. While Kape's history has raised concerns among some privacy advocates, the Deloitte audits specifically verify CyberGhost's independent operational practices. --- ## Streaming Performance Streaming is one of CyberGhost's standout features. It offers dedicated streaming-optimized servers labeled by platform and region, which makes unblocking geo-restricted content remarkably straightforward. ### Platform-by-Platform Results | Platform | Unblocked? | Notes | |----------|-----------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes | Worked reliably on dedicated US streaming servers. Full HD confirmed. | | Netflix Japan | Yes | Accessible via Japan streaming server. | | Netflix UK | Yes | UK library unblocked without issues. | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | Consistent access through UK servers. | | Disney+ | Yes | US and UK libraries tested. No buffering on WireGuard. | | Amazon Prime Video | Yes | US library accessible on first attempt. | | Hulu | Yes | Unblocked immediately on dedicated server. | | HBO Max (Max) | Yes | Smooth playback on US streaming servers. | | ESPN+ | Yes | Live sports streamed without interruption. | | Paramount+ | Yes | US content accessible. | | DAZN | Yes | Multiple regions tested successfully. | | Sling TV | Yes | US-only content accessible. | | RTL+ DE | Yes | German content library available. | | Peacock | Yes | US-only content unblocked. | | Crunchyroll | Yes | US library accessible for anime streaming. | CyberGhost unblocked **15 out of 15 streaming platforms** we tested. The dedicated streaming servers are clearly labeled in the app (e.g., "Netflix US," "BBC iPlayer"), so you do not need to guess which server to use. This is a significant usability advantage over competitors that require trial-and-error server selection. ### Smart DNS CyberGhost also offers a **Smart DNS** feature for devices that do not natively support VPN apps, such as gaming consoles, Apple TV, and older smart TVs. Smart DNS routes only DNS queries (not your full traffic), so it does not encrypt your connection, but it does unblock geo-restricted streaming content with zero speed impact. --- ## Pricing & Plans CyberGhost is one of the most affordable VPN services on the market, especially on longer-term plans. Here is the current pricing as of March 2026. ### Pricing Overview | Plan | Monthly Cost | Total Billed | Money-Back Guarantee | |------|-------------|-------------|---------------------| | **1 Month** | €11.99/mo | €11.99 | 14 days | | **6 Months** | €6.99/mo | €41.94 | 45 days | | **2 Years + 2 Months** | €2.19/mo | €56.94 | 45 days | All plans include identical features: full access to all 11,690+ servers, 7 simultaneous connections, unlimited bandwidth, and 24/7 customer support. The 26-month plan represents an 82% discount versus the monthly price. **Renewal pricing:** After the initial term, the 26-month plan renews at €56.94 per year. This is worth noting because many VPN providers increase renewal rates significantly — CyberGhost's renewal is relatively fair compared to competitors. ### Optional Add-Ons - **Dedicated IP:** €5/month (or €2.50/month on a 24-month commitment). Uses a token-based system verified by Deloitte so your IP cannot be linked to your account. - **CyberGhost Security Suite (Windows):** €4.50/month — includes antivirus and privacy tools. - **Password Manager:** Free basic version included; premium at €2.95/month. ### Payment Methods CyberGhost accepts credit/debit cards, PayPal, iDeal, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and BitPay (cryptocurrency). VAT applies based on your jurisdiction (21% in the Netherlands). ### Is It Worth the Price? At €2.19/month on the 26-month plan, CyberGhost is excellent value. It is cheaper than NordVPN (€3.39/month) while offering more servers and a longer money-back guarantee (45 days vs 30 days). However, Surfshark (€1.99/month) is slightly cheaper and offers unlimited simultaneous connections. CyberGhost justifies its price through its massive server network, NoSpy servers, and dedicated streaming servers.
💡 Pro Tip: The 45-day money-back guarantee on the 6-month and 26-month plans is the longest in the industry — don't sign up for the monthly plan unless you only need short-term access, since it drops to just 14 days. In our experience, the refund process took under 24 hours via live chat.
--- ## Apps & Usability CyberGhost offers native applications for every major platform, and the user experience is designed with beginners in mind while still providing enough depth for advanced users. ### Supported Platforms - **Desktop:** Windows 7+, macOS 10.13+, Linux (Ubuntu, Fedora, CentOS) - **Mobile:** iOS, Android - **Browser Extensions:** Chrome, Firefox - **TV & Streaming:** Android TV, Amazon Fire TV Stick - **Other:** Router support (DD-WRT, TomatoUSB), Smart DNS for gaming consoles and smart TVs ### Simultaneous Connections Every CyberGhost subscription allows **7 simultaneous device connections**. This is fewer than NordVPN (10) and far fewer than Surfshark (unlimited), but sufficient for most individuals and small households. Installing CyberGhost on your router covers all connected devices as a single connection. ### User Interface The CyberGhost app features a clean, dark-themed interface with a sidebar listing server categories: All Servers, NoSpy Servers, Streaming, Gaming, and Torrenting. Each category shows servers optimized for that specific use case with clear labels. The "Best Location" button connects you to the fastest available server automatically. Server load percentages are displayed next to each location, so you can avoid overloaded servers manually if you prefer. ### Smart Rules (Windows & Android) CyberGhost's Smart Rules feature lets you create automation: - Auto-connect on app launch - Auto-connect when joining specific WiFi networks - Auto-launch specific apps when VPN connects - Define split tunneling rules (which apps bypass the VPN) Split tunneling is only available on Windows and Android — it is absent on macOS and iOS, which is a notable limitation. ### Linux Support CyberGhost's Linux app is command-line only — there is no graphical interface. This works fine for experienced Linux users but puts it behind NordVPN, which now offers a full GUI on Linux. Supported distributions include Ubuntu, Fedora, and CentOS. --- ## What We Do Not Like No VPN is perfect, and CyberGhost has several drawbacks worth considering: 1. **Slower long-distance speeds.** Connecting to servers on another continent (especially Asia) results in dramatic speed drops. The Japan server delivered only 6.61 Mbps in our tests — barely enough for SD video. 2. **Does not work in China.** CyberGhost acknowledges it does not reliably bypass the Great Firewall. If you need a VPN for use in China, Russia, or other heavily censored countries, look at ExpressVPN or NordVPN instead. 3. **No port forwarding.** Torrent users who need port forwarding for optimal speeds will need to look elsewhere. CyberGhost removed this feature and has not brought it back. 4. **Kape Technologies ownership.** CyberGhost's parent company, Kape Technologies, has a controversial history (formerly Crossrider, which was associated with adware). While the Deloitte audits verify CyberGhost's independent practices, some privacy purists prefer providers with cleaner corporate histories, such as Mullvad or IVPN. 5. **No native Apple TV app.** While competitors like NordVPN and ExpressVPN have launched native Apple TV apps, CyberGhost still requires router setup or Smart DNS for Apple TV. 6. **Split tunneling missing on macOS/iOS.** This feature is only available on Windows and Android, which limits flexibility for Apple users. 7. **7 device limit.** In an era where Surfshark offers unlimited connections and NordVPN allows 10, CyberGhost's 7-device cap feels restrictive for larger households. --- ## Who Should Use CyberGhost? ### Best For - **Streaming enthusiasts on a budget** who want dedicated, labeled servers for Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and more — without guessing which server works - **VPN beginners** who want a simple, well-designed app that makes server selection effortless through categorized server lists - **Privacy-conscious users** who value independently audited no-logs policies and appreciate the transparency of quarterly reports - **European users** who benefit from CyberGhost's strong European server coverage and Romanian jurisdiction - **Torrent users** who need dedicated P2P-optimized servers (but do not require port forwarding) ### Not Ideal For - **Users in censored countries** — CyberGhost does not work reliably in China, Russia, or the UAE. Consider NordVPN or ExpressVPN instead. - **Speed-obsessed users** — If you have a gigabit connection and want maximum throughput, NordVPN's NordLynx delivers nearly 4x the speeds of CyberGhost. - **Large households** — With only 7 simultaneous connections, families with many devices should consider Surfshark (unlimited connections) instead. - **Extreme privacy advocates** — Those who want anonymous account creation with no email requirement should look at Mullvad VPN, which accepts cash payments. - **Linux power users** — The CLI-only Linux app lacks the GUI that NordVPN now provides. --- ## Frequently Asked Questions ### Is CyberGhost VPN safe to use in 2026? Yes. CyberGhost has completed three independent no-logs audits by Deloitte (2022, 2024, and Q4 2025), all conducted under ISAE 3000 international standards. Its servers run on RAM-only infrastructure, meaning all data is wiped on every reboot. The company publishes quarterly transparency reports showing that all legal requests (including 56,053 DMCA complaints in Q4 2025) were denied because no user data exists to provide. ### Does CyberGhost work with Netflix? Yes. In our March 2026 tests, CyberGhost unblocked Netflix US, Netflix UK, Netflix Japan, and over 12 other regional libraries. It offers dedicated streaming servers labeled by platform and region, which makes finding the right server effortless. Streaming quality was stable at Full HD on nearby servers using the WireGuard protocol. ### How many devices can I connect to CyberGhost at once? CyberGhost allows up to 7 simultaneous device connections on a single account. This covers Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, and browser extensions. If you need to protect more devices, installing CyberGhost on a compatible router (DD-WRT or TomatoUSB) covers your entire network as one connection. ### Is CyberGhost faster than NordVPN? No. In our 2026 speed tests, CyberGhost averaged 204 Mbps download on nearby servers, while NordVPN averaged 780 Mbps. NordVPN's proprietary NordLynx protocol significantly outperforms CyberGhost's WireGuard implementation. However, 200 Mbps is more than enough for HD streaming, video calls, and general browsing — you will only notice the difference on very fast connections or large downloads. ### Does CyberGhost have a free trial? CyberGhost does not offer a traditional free trial on desktop. However, the 6-month and 26-month plans include a **45-day money-back guarantee** — the longest in the VPN industry. The monthly plan has a shorter 14-day guarantee. You can test the full service risk-free and request a refund within the guarantee period. ### Does CyberGhost work in China? No. CyberGhost does not reliably bypass the Great Firewall of China and the company acknowledges this limitation. If you need a VPN for use in China, we recommend NordVPN (which offers obfuscated servers) or ExpressVPN (which has a strong track record in censored regions). --- ## Sources & References This review is based on our hands-on testing (March 2026) and verified against the following sources: 1. [CyberGhost VPN Official Website](https://cyberghostvpn.com) — Server network, pricing, and feature documentation 2. [Cybernews — CyberGhost VPN Review 2026](https://cybernews.com/best-vpn/cyberghost-vpn-review/) — Independent speed testing and streaming analysis 3. [Security.org — CyberGhost VPN Review 2026](https://www.security.org/vpn/cyberghost/review/) — Third-party privacy and security assessment
